abstract: Idea Theory develops a rigorous algebraic framework for 'ideas' as composable structures with identity, equivalence, and emergent properties. Beginning from elementary mathematical foundations, the theory extends naturally to interpretations across the social sciences, humanities, philosophy, and the problem of emergence, providing a unified formal language for describing how complex meaning arises from simpler constituents.

  4. \section{Definitions} — at least 6–10      \begin{definition} blocks, each followed by a      \begin{remark} (50–150 words) connecting it to the      informal antecedent and noting any deviation.
  5. \section{Main Results} — at least 5–8      \begin{theorem} blocks, each followed by a real      \begin{proof} of 200–600 words (not placeholders)      structured as numbered steps; interleave      \begin{lemma} declarations as needed and prove them      too.  Add at least 3 \begin{corollary} blocks with      proofs.
  6. \section{Worked Examples} — at least 3      \begin{example} blocks (200–400 words each) showing      the theorems in action.
  7. \section{Lean 4 Verification} — one or more      \begin{lstlisting}[language=Lean4] blocks reproducing      the actual headline theorem statements from      lean/IdeaTheory/Theorems*.lean, plus 300–600      words of accompanying prose explaining how the Lean      proof realizes the informal argument.
  8. \section{Discussion: What the Formalization Reveals} —      500–1000 words reflecting on what the formal proofs      clarify, simplify, sharpen, or refute about the informal      literature.  Cite at least 4 further works.
  9. \section{Open Problems} — 200–400 words listing 4–8      concrete open problems left by both the literature and      the formalization.
 10. \section{Notes and References} — bibliographic notes      guiding the reader to the most important sources cited.
Use \cref / \Cref throughout for cross-references; cite generously; ensure every theorem/definition/lemma carries a \label.  No placeholder TODOs in the final output.
